title: Sour Slovakian Knafeh Gratin
description: A comforting Slovakian dish featuring knafeh, a type of cheese-filled pastry. This recipe is a twist on the traditional Slovakian recipe, with a tangy and rich flavor profile.
instructions: In a large cazuela, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Slowly pour in the yogurt, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until it thickens, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the feta cheese, crumbled, and cook, stirring constantly, until melted and smooth.
Stir in the lemon juice, black pepper, and dried dill.
Add the knafeh, gently folding it into the sauce until coated.
Transfer the cazuela to the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the knafeh is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and season with salt to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Garnish with parsley and serve hot.
Transfer the cazuela to the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the knafeh is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Garnish with parsley and serve hot.
